phases.name: Fever-trail derivation model (pure logic, no MUI)
phases.description: Add the kill-rate-critical core: a pure deriveFeverTrail(history) model function that turns the already-fetched DeliveryMetricsHistory series into the fever-chart's (schedule-consumed x, buffer-consumed y) coordinates plus a green/amber/red zone per snapshot, with the forward-only/sparse/zero-scope guards that yield an empty trail (no bubble). This is FE-only — NO backend change, NO new endpoint, NO new DTO/parser field, NO new demo seeding: every input (totalWork/doneWork/remainingWork/date + deliveryDate/firstSnapshotDate) already ships on DeliveryMetricsHistory (Slice 1). The SPIKE (docs/feature/delivery-metrics/spike/findings.md, verdict WORKS) validated this exact derivation against the real data shape; this step promotes that throwaway math into a tested model.

steps.name: deriveFeverTrail: schedule-vs-buffer coordinates + green/amber/red zones + empty-trail guards
criteria: A new pure function deriveFeverTrail(history: DeliveryMetricsHistory): FeverTrail computes, per snapshot point with totalWork > 0, scheduleConsumed = clamp((point.date - firstSnapshotDate) / (deliveryDate - firstSnapshotDate), 0, 1) * 100 (x), remaining = remainingWork / totalWork * 100 (y), and a zone derived from deviation = remaining - (100 - scheduleConsumed): deviation <= GREEN_MAX (5) => green, <= AMBER_MAX (20) => amber, else red — the SPIKE-validated model (findings.md). Thresholds are UPPER_SNAKE_CASE module constants (baseline defaults; tunability OUT of scope per feature-delta)

An on-track delivery (work burns down roughly with schedule) produces a trail with NO red zone; a degrading at-risk delivery (buffer consumed ahead of schedule) produces a trail that enters red — asserted from two fixtures, matching milestone-5 scenarios 1 + 2

FOLDED empty-trail guards (sibling asserts in the same describe, each its own RED arm): firstSnapshotDate === null => { points: [], empty: true }; points === [] => empty; deliveryDate - firstSnapshotDate <= 0 (span non-positive) => empty; a point with totalWork === 0 is excluded (no divide-by-zero bubble), and an all-zero-scope history => empty — these are the milestone-5 scenario-3 'no bubble' arms
The returned FeverTrail exposes, per point, the snapshot date, x, y, and zone so the chart (Phase 02) and its animation can consume an ordered, MUI-free structure
steps.scenario_name: deriveFeverTrail maps the snapshot series onto schedule/buffer coordinates and green/amber/red zones, with no bubble on sparse data
steps.implementation_notes: NEW model file FeverTrail.ts next to DeliveryMetricsHistory.ts (single responsibility; NO MUI import — this is the testable core where the mutation kill-rate must land). Define `export type FeverZone = 'green' | 'amber' | 'red'`, `export type FeverPoint = { date: Date; x: number; y: number; zone: FeverZone }`, `export type FeverTrail = { points: FeverPoint[]; empty: boolean }`. Module consts: `const GREEN_MAX_DEVIATION = 5`, `const AMBER_MAX_DEVIATION = 20` (percentage-points behind the on-track diagonal). Implement with LINQ-style array methods (filter/map — immutability rule; no push/sort/mutation), early returns, max 2 nesting. classifyZone(deviation) is a small helper with early returns, NOT a nested ternary (S3358). Guards first: if (!history.firstSnapshotDate || history.points.length === 0) return { points: [], empty: true }; const span = history.deliveryDate.getTime() - history.firstSnapshotDate.getTime(); if (span <= 0) return empty. Then points.filter(p => p.totalWork > 0).map(...); empty = result.length === 0. clamp via Math.min/Math.max (no new dep). Round x/y for stable assertions only if needed — prefer asserting zone + ordering over exact decimals to keep tests robust. Build fixtures with a getMockDeliveryMetricsHistory factory (extend the existing model factory if one exists, else add one validated against the DeliveryMetricsHistory type — do NOT redefine the type in the test, do NOT as-cast). CRITICAL FIXTURE MANDATE (SPIKE findings.md 'edge cases' — prevents fixture theatre): build THREE fixtures and assert deriveFeverTrail across all of them — (a) on-track => trail has NO red (negative check); (b) DEGRADING => trail crosses ALL THREE zones in order green -> amber -> red (this is the ONLY fixture that exercises the amber/red boundary logic and the zone-progression mutants — milestone-5 scenario 1 'through green, amber, AND red'; a clean on-track trail stays all-green and WILL NOT touch amber/red, so this fixture is non-negotiable); (c) at-risk => buffer consumed ahead of schedule so the trail ENTERS red early (milestone-5 scenario 2). Without fixture (b) the amber threshold and the green->amber->red ordering go untested and the zone-boundary mutants survive. CI/Sonar: TS strict, never any (narrow unknown with guards); Biome clean; no comments (banned in tests too); UPPER_SNAKE_CASE consts; no magic numbers inline (the thresholds ARE the named consts).

phases.name: DeliveryFeverChart component — zone-coloured bubbles + animated date-ordered trail + empty state
phases.description: Render the fever trail as the net-new TameFlow visualization using the already-installed MUI-X 9.0.1 (ScatterChart / composed ChartContainer — NO new charting dependency, confirmed in the SPIKE). The load-bearing decision signal is which zone the LATEST bubble sits in; the animated traversal across snapshot dates (user greenlit, 2026-06-04) is the presentational layer on top. RTL mocks the chart primitive and asserts the BUSINESS mapping (trail points, per-bubble zone colour, target/diagonal reference, empty state), never pixels (the dotted-line lesson).

steps.name: DeliveryFeverChart renders the zone-coloured bubble trail with the on-track diagonal + empty state
criteria: A new DeliveryFeverChart consumes history, calls deriveFeverTrail, and plots one bubble per trail point at (x = schedule consumed %, y = buffer/remaining %), each bubble coloured by its zone (green/amber/red) via the theme palette (success/warning/error) — the latest bubble emphasised so 'which zone are we in now' is the at-a-glance read

The chart conveys the zones against the on-track diagonal: render the two zone-boundary references (the green/amber and amber/red diagonals, i.e. y = (100 - x) + GREEN_MAX and + AMBER_MAX) and/or a shaded zone background so a viewer can see the trail crossing from green toward red; axes are fixed 0-100% on both x (schedule consumed) and y (buffer remaining)

PRE-GATED rendering decision (decide BEFORE committing, not post-hoc): the zone boundaries render as EITHER explicit diagonal lineplots OR a legend/caption naming the zones — per-bubble zone colour is the non-negotiable signal either way. If the explicit diagonals are deferred within the timebox, record that deferral as a one-line DESIGN note in the slice mutation report; do not let the choice expand scope

When deriveFeverTrail(history).empty is true (no/sparse snapshots, zero-scope), the chart renders the SAME forward-only empty-state message idiom as DeliveryBurnupChart ('builds forward from today — no snapshots recorded yet') and NO bubble — milestone-5 scenario 3
The component root carries data-testid='delivery-fever-chart' for the E2E POM, mirroring delivery-burnup-chart
steps.scenario_name: The fever chart plots the zone-coloured bubble trail and shows an empty state on sparse data
steps.implementation_notes: NEW component modelled on DeliveryBurnupChart.tsx's shell (Card/CardContent/Typography title, useTheme, the SAME FORWARD_ONLY_EMPTY_STATE empty-card branch when trail.empty). Rendering: MUI-X 9.0.1 ScatterChart for the bubbles (confirmed installed; SPIKE). Colour each bubble by zone using theme.palette.success.main / warning.main / error.main — map the FeverZone to a colour via a small module helper with early returns (NOT a nested ternary in a JSX slot — S3358). For the zone-boundary diagonals, prefer composing a ChartContainer with a LinePlot (two 2-point boundary series from (0, 100+GREEN_MAX)->(100, GREEN_MAX) style geometry) UNDER a ScatterPlot of the bubbles; if composing LinePlot+ScatterPlot proves fiddly within the timebox, fall back to colouring bubbles by zone + a visible legend/caption naming the zones (bubble colour is the non-negotiable signal; the diagonal bands are the enhancement) and record the fallback in the slice mutation/notes. xAxis/yAxis fixed min:0 max:100 with a '% schedule consumed' / '% work remaining' label. RTL: MOCK the MUI chart primitive (ScatterChart/ChartContainer) as the other chart tests do and assert the mapped series objects — the bubble dataset length, each bubble's zone->colour, the boundary references, and the empty-state branch from fixtures — assert BUSINESS behaviour, NOT 'renders without crashing', NOT pixels. Reuse getMockDeliveryMetricsHistory fixtures from 01-01. TS strict, no any; options-object if any helper > 3 params (S107); Biome clean; no comments; immutable series construction (build arrays via map, no push).

steps.name: Animate the trail: progressive date-ordered reveal of the bubbles
criteria: The bubble trail animates across snapshot dates: on mount the chart reveals the trail points in date order over time (the 'fever' trail building up), ending on the full trail with the latest bubble emphasised — milestone-5 scenario 1 'the trail animates across snapshot dates'

The reveal logic is a pure, fake-timer-testable unit (e.g. a useFeverTrailAnimation(pointCount) hook returning the current visibleCount that advances from 1 to pointCount) — a unit test with fake timers asserts visibleCount progresses in order and STOPS at pointCount (never overshoots, never restarts), and the chart renders trail.points.slice(0, visibleCount)
Animation is inert on the empty/sparse trail (pointCount 0 => nothing animates, no timer leak) and on a single-point trail (immediately complete); the timer is cleared on unmount
Reduced-motion / determinism: the animation does not depend on an unstable inline value (no inline new Date()/Math.random() in an effect dependency) so it cannot trigger a re-render loop
steps.scenario_name: The fever trail reveals its bubbles in date order and settles on the full trail
steps.implementation_notes: Isolate the timer in a NEW hook useFeverTrailAnimation(pointCount: number): number (visibleCount). Implement with a single setInterval/clearInterval in a useEffect whose ONLY dependency is pointCount (a primitive number — stable, no object/Date dep). REACT #185 LESSON (memory project-react185-loop): never feed an inline new Date()/fresh object into a useEffect dep; here the dep is a primitive count so the loop trap is structurally avoided — keep it that way and clear the interval in the cleanup. Guard pointCount <= 1 (return pointCount immediately, no timer). The chart renders deriveFeverTrail(history).points.slice(0, visibleCount). Unit-test the hook with vi.useFakeTimers(): mount with pointCount=4, advance timers, assert visibleCount goes 1->2->3->4 and then STAYS 4 (kills the 'off-by-one / keeps incrementing' mutants); pointCount=0 => stays 0 and registers no pending timer; unmount mid-animation clears the interval (assert no act warning / no further advance). Keep the interval delay a named UPPER_SNAKE_CASE const. Assertion strategy: assert the visibleCount sequence (pure hook output) and that the chart's rendered bubble dataset length equals visibleCount from a fixture — NOT MUI frame internals, animation frame counts, or CSS selectors. TS strict no any; Biome clean; no comments; S3776 keep the hook simple (early return for the <=1 case).

phases.name: Wire the fever chart into the Metrics tab + walking-skeleton E2E
phases.description: Surface DeliveryFeverChart on the per-delivery Metrics tab beside the burnup and predictability charts (same already-fetched DeliveryMetricsHistory, no second request), and prove the end-to-end seam with ONE assertion in the existing walking-skeleton spec against seeded demo data. NO new demo seeding — SeedBurnupSnapshots already records remainingWork/doneWork/dates across the demo days, which is exactly what deriveFeverTrail consumes.

steps.name: Render DeliveryFeverChart in the Metrics tab + extend the walking-skeleton spec (run locally)
criteria: The Metrics tab in DeliverySection renders DeliveryFeverChart alongside DeliveryBurnupChart and DeliveryPredictabilityChart, fed from the same metricsHistory state (no second fetch); DeliverySection.metrics.test.tsx asserts all three charts are present
The existing DeliveryMetrics walking-skeleton spec asserts the fever chart is visible on the demo delivery's Metrics tab and that a bubble renders (the demo snapshots produce a non-empty trail)
The fever chart inherits the existing community/RBAC posture of the Metrics tab unchanged — no new gate (the Slice-1 D4 correction made delivery metrics community; do NOT re-add RequirePremium)
The spec runs GREEN against the locally started app with seeded demo data (Apollo Release / scenario 0), driven from loadDemoScenario, never live syncs
steps.scenario_name: Forecaster sees the fever chart with a bubble trail on the demo delivery's Metrics tab
steps.implementation_notes: In MetricsTab (DeliverySection.tsx) add <DeliveryFeverChart history={history} /> to the existing grid beside the burnup + predictability charts (all consume the already-fetched DeliveryMetricsHistory — no new getMetricsHistory call). Update the grid template if a third chart warrants it (keep responsive xs:'1fr' lg two-up; a third chart can span — keep it simple, no premature layout abstraction). DeliverySection.metrics.test.tsx: extend the existing 'both charts present' assertion to all three (assert the fever chart testid alongside burnup + predictability). E2E: EXTEND the existing @walking_skeleton DeliveryMetrics spec (VERIFIED to exist: Lighthouse.EndToEndTests/tests/specs/portfolios/DeliveryMetrics.spec.ts:12 carries `test('@walking_skeleton @US-01 forecaster opens a delivery's Metrics tab ...')` — extend that case, do NOT add a new spec file — E2E-minimalism; the zone/animation logic is fully covered by 01-01/02-01/02-02 unit+RTL, so the E2E only proves the seam). Extend the DeliveryMetricsTab POM with a `feverChart` getter (locator [data-testid='delivery-fever-chart']) and a bubble locator (ScatterChart marks render as path/circle under the chart — pick the stable selector by inspecting the rendered DOM when you RUN it) — ALL locators via the POM, never inline page.locator (E2E POM rule). Assert the fever chart is visible and at least one bubble mark is drawn. NO new demo seeding: SeedBurnupSnapshots (DemoDataService.cs ~:134) already writes remainingWork/doneWork per demo day, which deriveFeverTrail consumes; confirm by running. RUN the spec locally (dotnet run backend + loadDemoScenario + playwright) before committing — pnpm build only type-checks, not locator validity; do not punt to the user (e2e-run-locally-by-user rule). Deliveries are COMMUNITY so no license import needed. CI/Sonar on the FE wiring: TS strict no any; Biome clean; no comments.

cross_cutting.rbac: N/A new — read view; inherits the existing portfolio read path; UI gating via useRbac(); authorization via IRbacAdministrationService; no new write surface; no /my-summary fetch. The fever chart is a COMMUNITY feature on the SAME Metrics tab as the burnup/predictability charts: the Slice-1 D4 CORRECTION (shipped, CI-green, commit 554f4896) dropped RequirePremium from the metrics-history endpoint + the Metrics tab. Do NOT re-gate. (wave-decisions.md / feature-delta D4 'premium gating inherited' lines are STALE — follow the shipped community code, per the Slice-4 H2 adjudication.)
cross_cutting.lighthouse_clients: N/A — no new endpoint and no new contract. The fever chart is visualization-only, derived entirely client-side (deriveFeverTrail) from the existing DeliveryMetricsHistory series (totalWork/doneWork/remainingWork/date + deliveryDate/firstSnapshotDate) shipped since Slice 1 on the already-version-gated metrics-history endpoint (ADR-050). No server field, no FEATURE_REQUIRES_SERVER_NEWER_THAN bump. A CLI/MCP fever-status command is not applicable (no status endpoint).
cross_cutting.website: OPTIONAL marketing flourish under the delivery-tracking launch (slice doc). Deferred — N/A at this slice; revisit at the epic's website pass. No website action now.

validation.reviewer: nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer — CONDITIONALLY APPROVED on 2026-06-04; 1 blocker + 1 high + 2 nitpicks all adjudicated and fixed; spec path verified; re-approved by author same day
validation.notes: Slice-5 roadmap (US-05 fever chart, ADO #5154 — D9 STRETCH, greenlit 2026-06-04) generated from slice-05-fever-chart-stretch.md + milestone-5-fever-chart.feature + the SPIKE findings (docs/feature/delivery-metrics/spike/findings.md, verdict WORKS). KEY FACTS: (1) FE-ONLY slice — no backend, no migration, no new endpoint, no DTO/parser change, no new demo seeding; every input already ships on DeliveryMetricsHistory (Slice 1) and SeedBurnupSnapshots already records the trajectory. (2) The SPIKE de-risked the derivation math (schedule-vs-buffer coords + green/amber/red deviation-from-on-track-diagonal zones, thresholds 5/20pp) and confirmed MUI-X 9.0.1 ScatterChart is installed (no new dep). (3) Promotion gate = DISCARD-then-DELIVER (no standalone walking-skeleton commit; the e2e path mirrors the shipped Slice-3/4 wiring). (4) User greenlit ANIMATION in the committed slice (02-02), isolated in a fake-timer-testable hook to contain the React #185 trap (primitive-only effect dep). 4 steps / 3 phases: 01 pure deriveFeverTrail model (mutation-critical core; on-track=>no-red, at-risk=>red, sparse/zero-scope=>empty), 02 DeliveryFeverChart (zone-coloured bubbles + diagonal zone references + empty state, then animated date-ordered reveal), 03 Metrics-tab wiring + one walking-skeleton E2E assertion run locally. SPIKE CONSTRAINT carried to DISTILL/review: milestone-5 scenario 1 ('through green, amber, AND red') needs a fixture that degrades through all three bands — a clean on-track fixture stays all-green. REVIEW ADJUDICATION (nw-acceptance-designer-reviewer, all accepted): BLOCKER (three-band degrading fixture only implied in 01-01) FIXED -> 01-01 notes now carry a CRITICAL FIXTURE MANDATE for three fixtures (on-track no-red / degrading green->amber->red / at-risk early-red). HIGH (02-02 'do not over-assert MUI' framed as prose) FIXED -> reworded as an explicit assertion strategy (assert visibleCount sequence + bubble dataset length, not frame internals). NITPICK (02-01 zone-boundary fallback decided post-hoc) FIXED -> added a PRE-GATED rendering-decision criterion (explicit diagonals OR a zone legend, decided before commit, deferral noted in the mutation report). NITPICK (03-01 spec path unverified) FIXED -> verified DeliveryMetrics.spec.ts:12 carries the @walking_skeleton case; 03-01 extends it. Status APPROVED — ready for DELIVER execute-all.
